technology.description: The AKS Checklist is a Preview family with 167 normalized items, 2 high-severity findings, covers Application, Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery, Cluster Security, and touches services like Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS).
technology.whatThisMeans: Use to enrich design reviews, but validate recommendations before treating them as baseline controls.

quality.label: Preview · Limited confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: Many applications running for long periods of time eventually transition to broken states, and cannot recover except by being restarted. Kubernetes provides liveness probes to detect and remedy such situations. The probe is here to tell Kubernetes to restart your pod when it is not responding anymore
items.description: Many applications running for long periods of time eventually transition to broken states, and cannot recover except by being restarted. Kubernetes provides liveness probes to detect and remedy such situations. The probe is here to tell Kubernetes to restart your pod when it is not responding anymore

items.text: Protect slow starting containers with startup probes. Startup probe allow to delay the initial check by liveness which could cause deadlock or wrong result
items.description: Protect slow starting containers with startup probes. Startup probe allow to delay the initial check by liveness which could cause deadlock or wrong result

items.text: Sometimes, applications are temporarily unable to serve traffic. For example, an application might need to load large data or configuration files during startup, or depend on external services after startup. In such cases, you don't want to kill the application, but you don't want to send it requests either. Kubernetes provides readiness probes to detect and mitigate these situations. A pod with containers reporting that they are not ready does not receive traffic through Kubernetes Services.
items.description: Sometimes, applications are temporarily unable to serve traffic. For example, an application might need to load large data or configuration files during startup, or depend on external services after startup. In such cases, you don't want to kill the application, but you don't want to send it requests either. Kubernetes provides readiness probes to detect and mitigate these situations. A pod with containers reporting that they are not ready does not receive traffic through Kubernetes Services.

items.text: This hook is called immediately before a container is terminated due to an API request or management event such as liveness probe failure, preemption, resource contention and others. It can be used when you have critical process you want to finish or save when your pod is destroyed for any reason
items.description: This hook is called immediately before a container is terminated due to an API request or management event such as liveness probe failure, preemption, resource contention and others. It can be used when you have critical process you want to finish or save when your pod is destroyed for any reason

items.text: Ensure that your application always configure proper replicas to ensure resiliency in the event of a pod crashing or being evicted.
items.description: Ensure that your application always configure proper replicas to ensure resiliency in the event of a pod crashing or being evicted.

items.text: Ensure that your components are tagged, it could be business, security or technical tags and these tags will help to assess or apply relevant policies.
items.description: Ensure that your components are tagged, it could be business, security or technical tags and these tags will help to assess or apply relevant policies.

items.text: Automatically scale your application to the number of pods required to handle the current load. This can be achieved by using Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for CPU & Memory or by using KEDA for scaling based on other sources
items.description: Automatically scale your application to the number of pods required to handle the current load. This can be achieved by using Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for CPU & Memory or by using KEDA for scaling based on other sources

items.text: Secrets are not encrypted in etcd, prefer to store your secrets in a proper HSM like Azure Key Vault. You can then inject secrets using CSI provider.
items.description: Secrets are not encrypted in etcd, prefer to store your secrets in a proper HSM like Azure Key Vault. You can then inject secrets using CSI provider.

items.text: Don't use fixed credentials within pods or container images, as they are at risk of exposure or abuse. Instead, use workload identity federation to access Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) protected resources without needing to manage secrets
items.description: Don't use fixed credentials within pods or container images, as they are at risk of exposure or abuse. Instead, use workload identity federation to access Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) protected resources without needing to manage secrets

items.text: Namespaces give you the ability to create logical partitions and enforce separation of your resources as well as limit the scope of user permissions. Don't forget not to use the Default namespace
items.description: Namespaces give you the ability to create logical partitions and enforce separation of your resources as well as limit the scope of user permissions. Don't forget not to use the Default namespace

items.text: When Containers have resource requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place Pods on. And when Containers have their limits specified, contention for resources on a node can be handled in a specified manner.
items.description: When Containers have resource requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place Pods on. And when Containers have their limits specified, contention for resources on a node can be handled in a specified manner.

items.text: A security context defines privilege and access control settings for a Pod or Container. Control the capabilities and the rights your container can have. If you don't specify the security context, the pod get the 'default' one which may have more rights that it should
items.description: A security context defines privilege and access control settings for a Pod or Container. Control the capabilities and the rights your container can have. If you don't specify the security context, the pod get the 'default' one which may have more rights that it should

items.text: Best practices inside the cluster start with the configuration of your manifests. Ensure that they respect good practices
items.description: Best practices inside the cluster start with the configuration of your manifests. Ensure that they respect good practices

items.text: Define a Image build security baseline for your developers to follow. You should also use scanning tools to detect Dockerfile issue
items.description: Define a Image build security baseline for your developers to follow. You should also use scanning tools to detect Dockerfile issue

items.text: Introduction of DevSecOps into the environment to promote a proactive security model that starts to shift the responsibility left
items.description: Introduction of DevSecOps into the environment to promote a proactive security model that starts to shift the responsibility left

items.text: Restrict builds with identified issues.
items.description: Restrict builds with identified issues.

items.text: Being able to assess and restrict the Compliance state of an image on build.
items.description: Being able to assess and restrict the Compliance state of an image on build.

items.text: The App Containerization tool offers a point-and-containerize approach to repackage applications as containers with minimal to no code changes by using the running state of the application. The tool currently supports containerizing ASP.NET applications and Java web applications running on Apache Tomcat.
items.description: The App Containerization tool offers a point-and-containerize approach to repackage applications as containers with minimal to no code changes by using the running state of the application. The tool currently supports containerizing ASP.NET applications and Java web applications running on Apache Tomcat.

items.text: There are a variety of techniques to deploy new applications to production so choosing the right strategy is an important decision that needs to be made to leverage the impact of change on the consumer.
items.description: There are a variety of techniques to deploy new applications to production so choosing the right strategy is an important decision that needs to be made to leverage the impact of change on the consumer.

items.text: Naked pods are pods not linked to a Replicaset or a Deployment. Naked Pods will not be rescheduled in the event of a node failure.
items.description: Naked pods are pods not linked to a Replicaset or a Deployment. Naked Pods will not be rescheduled in the event of a node failure.

items.text: If this attributes is not properly define kubernetes downloads the container image for each new instance of the containers.
items.description: If this attributes is not properly define kubernetes downloads the container image for each new instance of the containers.

items.text: If this attributes is not properly define kubernetes downloads the container image for each new instance of the containers.
items.description: If this attributes is not properly define kubernetes downloads the container image for each new instance of the containers.

items.text: This will help ensure your workloads remain online during cluster upgrades.
items.description: This will help ensure your workloads remain online during cluster upgrades.

items.text: This will help ensure your workloads remain online during cluster upgrades.
items.description: This will help ensure your workloads remain online during cluster upgrades.

items.text: Before going to production, it is very important to decide the target of availability and recovery time you are looking for. This will help you to decide the right backup strategy but also the architecture of your solution.
items.description: Before going to production, it is very important to decide the target of availability and recovery time you are looking for. This will help you to decide the right backup strategy but also the architecture of your solution.

items.text: A whitespace (greenfield) deployment is the exercise to delete everything and to redeploy the whole platform in an automated way.
items.description: A whitespace (greenfield) deployment is the exercise to delete everything and to redeploy the whole platform in an automated way.

items.text: An Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster distributes resources such as the nodes and storage across logical sections of the underlying Azure compute infrastructure. This deployment model makes sure that the nodes run across separate update and fault domains in a single Azure datacenter.
items.description: An Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster distributes resources such as the nodes and storage across logical sections of the underlying Azure compute infrastructure. This deployment model makes sure that the nodes run across separate update and fault domains in a single Azure datacenter.
items.graph: resources | where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend compliant= isnotnull(properties.agentPoolProfiles[0].availabilityZones) | distinct id,compliant

items.text: When you deploy multiple AKS clusters, choose regions where AKS is available, and use paired regions.
items.description: When you deploy multiple AKS clusters, choose regions where AKS is available, and use paired regions.

items.text: Azure Traffic Manager can direct customers to their closest AKS cluster and application instance. For the best performance and redundancy, direct all application traffic through Traffic Manager before it goes to your AKS cluster.
items.description: Azure Traffic Manager can direct customers to their closest AKS cluster and application instance. For the best performance and redundancy, direct all application traffic through Traffic Manager before it goes to your AKS cluster.

items.text: Your applications might use Azure Storage for their data. Because your applications are spread across multiple AKS clusters in different regions, you need to keep the storage synchronized
items.description: Your applications might use Azure Storage for their data. Because your applications are spread across multiple AKS clusters in different regions, you need to keep the storage synchronized

items.text: standard-tier (formerly Uptime SLA) is an optional feature to enable a financially backed, higher SLA for a cluster. It provides you a 99,95% SLA instead of the 99,5% SLO and is relevant for your production (and large) clusters
items.description: standard-tier (formerly Uptime SLA) is an optional feature to enable a financially backed, higher SLA for a cluster. It provides you a 99,95% SLA instead of the 99,5% SLO and is relevant for your production (and large) clusters

items.text: Even if you run several copies of your Pods, there are no guarantees that losing a node won't take down your service.
items.description: Even if you run several copies of your Pods, there are no guarantees that losing a node won't take down your service.

items.text: Companies that want a local presence, or a hot backup, choose to run services from multiple Azure regions.
items.description: Companies that want a local presence, or a hot backup, choose to run services from multiple Azure regions.

items.text: Use Azure availability zones to create a resilient and high availability Azure container registry within an Azure region.
items.description: Use Azure availability zones to create a resilient and high availability Azure container registry within an Azure region.

items.text: Because container registries are resources that are used across multiple container hosts, a registry should reside in its own resource group.
items.description: Because container registries are resources that are used across multiple container hosts, a registry should reside in its own resource group.

items.text: Once you enable the soft delete policy, ACR manages the deleted artifacts as the soft deleted artifacts with a set retention period. Thereby you have ability to list, filter, and restore the soft deleted artifacts. Once the retention period is complete, all the soft deleted artifacts are auto-purged.
items.description: Once you enable the soft delete policy, ACR manages the deleted artifacts as the soft deleted artifacts with a set retention period. Thereby you have ability to list, filter, and restore the soft deleted artifacts. Once the retention period is complete, all the soft deleted artifacts are auto-purged.

items.text: Use logical isolation to separate teams and projects. Try to minimize the number of physical AKS clusters you deploy to isolate teams or applications
items.description: Use logical isolation to separate teams and projects. Try to minimize the number of physical AKS clusters you deploy to isolate teams or applications

items.text: Minimize the use of physical isolation for each separate team or application deployment
items.description: Minimize the use of physical isolation for each separate team or application deployment

items.text: With Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), you can set Azure tags on an AKS cluster and its related resources by using Azure Resource Manager, through the Azure CLI.
items.description: With Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), you can set Azure tags on an AKS cluster and its related resources by using Azure Resource Manager, through the Azure CLI.

items.text: Mariner Container-Optimized OS is a first-party operating system developed specifically for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). It is designed to optimize the execution of Kubernetes workloads, offering a secure, lightweight, and highly performant environment.
items.description: Mariner Container-Optimized OS is a first-party operating system developed specifically for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). It is designed to optimize the execution of Kubernetes workloads, offering a secure, lightweight, and highly performant environment.

items.text: Some industries require certified kubernetes or to implement specific configurations. AKS offers several features to meet this requirements
items.description: Some industries require certified kubernetes or to implement specific configurations. AKS offers several features to meet this requirements

items.text: Starting with Kubernetes version 1.19, AKS will no longer allow the managed Kubernetes dashboard add-on to be installed for security reasons, and the add-on is scheduled to be deprecated. Ensure the Kubernetes dashboard is not installed on the cluster.
items.description: Starting with Kubernetes version 1.19, AKS will no longer allow the managed Kubernetes dashboard add-on to be installed for security reasons, and the add-on is scheduled to be deprecated. Ensure the Kubernetes dashboard is not installed on the cluster.

items.text: By default, ETCD is encrypted at rest with keys managed by Microsoft. It is possible to encrypt the database using your own key using a KMS plugin and store the key in Azure Key Vault.
items.description: By default, ETCD is encrypted at rest with keys managed by Microsoft. It is possible to encrypt the database using your own key using a KMS plugin and store the key in Azure Key Vault.

items.text: To stay current on new features and bug fixes, regularly upgrade to the Kubernetes version in your AKS cluster. Support for kubernetes is current and N-2 versions only
items.description: To stay current on new features and bug fixes, regularly upgrade to the Kubernetes version in your AKS cluster. Support for kubernetes is current and N-2 versions only

items.text: You can now protect your Kubernetes clusters and container workloads from potential threats by restricting deployment of container images with vulnerabilities in their software components.
items.description: You can now protect your Kubernetes clusters and container workloads from potential threats by restricting deployment of container images with vulnerabilities in their software components.

items.text: Use Azure Key Vault to store Secrets and Certificates
items.description: Use Azure Key Vault to store Secrets and Certificates

items.text: Security Center brings security benefits to your AKS clusters using data already gathered by the AKS master node.
items.description: Security Center brings security benefits to your AKS clusters using data already gathered by the AKS master node.

items.text: With image cleaner, you can detect and automatically remove all unused and vulnerable images cached on AKS nodes keeping the nodes cleaner and safer.
items.description: With image cleaner, you can detect and automatically remove all unused and vulnerable images cached on AKS nodes keeping the nodes cleaner and safer.

items.text: Microsoft Defender for Containers provides protections for your Kubernetes clusters wherever they're running (AKS and on-premesis)
items.description: Microsoft Defender for Containers provides protections for your Kubernetes clusters wherever they're running (AKS and on-premesis)

items.text: Azure Policy integrates with the Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) to apply at-scale enforcements and safeguards on your clusters in a centralized, consistent manner.
items.description: Azure Policy integrates with the Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) to apply at-scale enforcements and safeguards on your clusters in a centralized, consistent manner.
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(properties.addonProfiles.azurepolicy) and properties.addonProfiles.azurepolicy.enabled==true) | distinct id,compliant

items.text: Manage system node pools in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and add taint to your system nodepool to make it dedicated
items.description: Manage system node pools in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and add taint to your system nodepool to make it dedicated

items.text: You may want to update, or rotate, the credentials as part of a defined security polic
items.description: You may want to update, or rotate, the credentials as part of a defined security polic

items.text: Azure Container Registry allows you to build, store, and manage container images and artifacts in a private registry
items.description: Azure Container Registry allows you to build, store, and manage container images and artifacts in a private registry

items.text: Microsoft Defender for Containers is the cloud-native solution that is used to secure your containers
items.description: Microsoft Defender for Containers is the cloud-native solution that is used to secure your containers

items.text: Containers run within a Trusted Execution Environment(TEE) brings isolation from other containers, the node kernel in a hardware protected, integrity protected attestable environment.
items.description: Containers run within a Trusted Execution Environment(TEE) brings isolation from other containers, the node kernel in a hardware protected, integrity protected attestable environment.

items.text: Plan for multi-tenant clusters and separation of resources and use logical or physical isolation in your AKS clusters
items.description: Plan for multi-tenant clusters and separation of resources and use logical or physical isolation in your AKS clusters

items.text: Plan for multi-tenant clusters and separation of resources and use logical or physical isolation in your AKS clusters
items.description: Plan for multi-tenant clusters and separation of resources and use logical or physical isolation in your AKS clusters

items.text: Scan your container images to ensure there are no vulnerabilities in it
items.description: Scan your container images to ensure there are no vulnerabilities in it

items.text: One of the most common custom policies that you might want to consider is to restrict the images that can be deployed in your cluster. But it can also be addressed with a proper egress lockdown or using an admission controller
items.description: One of the most common custom policies that you might want to consider is to restrict the images that can be deployed in your cluster. But it can also be addressed with a proper egress lockdown or using an admission controller

items.text: Integrate Runtime Security for your pods. To complete the defense in depth structure, ensure Runtime protection is in place to protect from process, network, storage and system call attacks.
items.description: Integrate Runtime Security for your pods. To complete the defense in depth structure, ensure Runtime protection is in place to protect from process, network, storage and system call attacks.

items.text: Use policy to protect images from drift while in the registry, on both push and pull.
items.description: Use policy to protect images from drift while in the registry, on both push and pull.

items.text: The Azure Container Registry service supports a set of built-in Azure roles that provide different levels of permissions to an Azure container registry. Use Azure role-based access control (RBAC) to assign specific permissions to users, service principals, or other identities that need to interact with a registry.
items.description: The Azure Container Registry service supports a set of built-in Azure roles that provide different levels of permissions to an Azure container registry. Use Azure role-based access control (RBAC) to assign specific permissions to users, service principals, or other identities that need to interact with a registry.

items.text: Limit access to a registry by assigning virtual network private IP addresses to the registry endpoints and using Azure Private Link. Network traffic between the clients on the virtual network and the registry's private endpoints traverses the virtual network and a private link on the Microsoft backbone network, eliminating exposure from the public internet. Private Link also enables enables private registry access from on-premises through Azure ExpressRoute private peering or a VPN gateway.
items.description: Limit access to a registry by assigning virtual network private IP addresses to the registry endpoints and using Azure Private Link. Network traffic between the clients on the virtual network and the registry's private endpoints traverses the virtual network and a private link on the Microsoft backbone network, eliminating exposure from the public internet. Private Link also enables enables private registry access from on-premises through Azure ExpressRoute private peering or a VPN gateway.

items.text: When building a docker image, try to use the distroless version of the base OS image, to reduce the risk of vulnerabilities with preinstalled but unused tools. From example, use base-debian10 instead of debian10
items.description: When building a docker image, try to use the distroless version of the base OS image, to reduce the risk of vulnerabilities with preinstalled but unused tools. From example, use base-debian10 instead of debian10

items.text: As you use base images for application images, use automation to build new images when the base image is updated. As those base images typically include security fixes, update any downstream application container images.
items.description: As you use base images for application images, use automation to build new images when the base image is updated. As those base images typically include security fixes, update any downstream application container images.

items.text: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) can be configured to use Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) for user authentication. In this configuration, you can sign in to an AKS cluster by using your Azure AD authentication token.
items.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) can be configured to use Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) for user authentication. In this configuration, you can sign in to an AKS cluster by using your Azure AD authentication token.
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend compliant = isnotnull(properties.aadProfile) | distinct id,compliant

items.text: Control access to cluster resources using Kubernetes role-based access control and Azure Active Directory identities in Azure Kubernetes Service
items.description: Control access to cluster resources using Kubernetes role-based access control and Azure Active Directory identities in Azure Kubernetes Service

items.text: AKS can authenticate to ACR without using any password, but by using either Service Principal or Managed Identity.
items.description: AKS can authenticate to ACR without using any password, but by using either Service Principal or Managed Identity.

items.text: Each AKS cluster needs either a Managed Identity or Service Principal. We recommend using Managed Identity in AKS
items.description: Each AKS cluster needs either a Managed Identity or Service Principal. We recommend using Managed Identity in AKS

items.text: To limit who can get that Kubernetes configuration (kubeconfig) information and to limit the permissions they then have, you can use Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC).
items.description: To limit who can get that Kubernetes configuration (kubeconfig) information and to limit the permissions they then have, you can use Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC).

items.text: You can use kubelogin to access the cluster with non-interactive service principal sign-in.
items.description: You can use kubelogin to access the cluster with non-interactive service principal sign-in.

items.text: AKS offers users the ability to disable local accounts via a flag, disable-local-accounts
items.description: AKS offers users the ability to disable local accounts via a flag, disable-local-accounts

items.text: AKS offers users the ability to enable access for a limited time
items.description: AKS offers users the ability to enable access for a limited time

items.text: A Kubelet identity enables access granted to the existing identity prior to cluster creation.
items.description: A Kubelet identity enables access granted to the existing identity prior to cluster creation.

items.text: When integrating Azure AD with your AKS cluster, you can also use Conditional Access to control access to your cluster.
items.description: When integrating Azure AD with your AKS cluster, you can also use Conditional Access to control access to your cluster.

items.text: For integration with existing virtual networks or on-premises networks, use Azure CNI networking in AKS. This network model also allows greater separation of resources and controls in an enterprise environment but be aware of the impact on the network topology/IP ranges. You can now use the CNI overlay model which gives more control and takes the benefits of both approachs.
items.description: For integration with existing virtual networks or on-premises networks, use Azure CNI networking in AKS. This network model also allows greater separation of resources and controls in an enterprise environment but be aware of the impact on the network topology/IP ranges. You can now use the CNI overlay model which gives more control and takes the benefits of both approachs.

items.text: The size of your virtual network and its subnet must accommodate the number of pods you plan to run and the number of nodes for the cluster.
items.description: The size of your virtual network and its subnet must accommodate the number of pods you plan to run and the number of nodes for the cluster.

items.text: To distribute HTTP or HTTPS traffic to your applications, use ingress resources and controllers. Ingress controllers provide additional features over a regular Azure load balancer, and can be managed as native Kubernetes resources.
items.description: To distribute HTTP or HTTPS traffic to your applications, use ingress resources and controllers. Ingress controllers provide additional features over a regular Azure load balancer, and can be managed as native Kubernetes resources.

items.text: If you plan to host exposed applications, to scan incoming traffic for potential attacks, use a web application firewall (WAF) such as Barracuda WAF for Azure or Azure Application Gateway. These more advanced network resources can also route traffic beyond just HTTP and HTTPS connections or basic SSL termination.
items.description: If you plan to host exposed applications, to scan incoming traffic for potential attacks, use a web application firewall (WAF) such as Barracuda WAF for Azure or Azure Application Gateway. These more advanced network resources can also route traffic beyond just HTTP and HTTPS connections or basic SSL termination.

items.text: When a user creates an Ingress manifest, they can use any hostname in it. You may want to control which hostnames are allowed to use, like your company's hostnames.
items.description: When a user creates an Ingress manifest, they can use any hostname in it. You may want to control which hostnames are allowed to use, like your company's hostnames.

items.text: There is almost no reason to directly expose the ingress entry point to Internet but by default AKS create a public one. Tell him to create an internal one only.
items.description: There is almost no reason to directly expose the ingress entry point to Internet but by default AKS create a public one. Tell him to create an internal one only.

items.text: Use network policies to allow or deny traffic to pods. By default, all traffic is allowed between pods within a cluster. For improved security, define rules that limit pod communication.
items.description: Use network policies to allow or deny traffic to pods. By default, all traffic is allowed between pods within a cluster. For improved security, define rules that limit pod communication.

items.text: Start by creating a deny all policy in each namespace and then add specific policies.
items.description: Start by creating a deny all policy in each namespace and then add specific policies.

items.text: Use Azure Firewall to secure and control all egress traffic going outside of the cluster.
items.description: Use Azure Firewall to secure and control all egress traffic going outside of the cluster.

items.text: When possible, use private link to only allow private network to reach your registry.
items.description: When possible, use private link to only allow private network to reach your registry.

items.text: By default, Pods have access to VMSS IMDS and can request access token from the attached Managed Identity. This access should be restriced by using Network Policy.
items.description: By default, Pods have access to VMSS IMDS and can request access token from the attached Managed Identity. This access should be restriced by using Network Policy.

items.text: In a private cluster, the control plane or API server has internal IP addresses and is not exposed to Internet
items.description: In a private cluster, the control plane or API server has internal IP addresses and is not exposed to Internet

items.text: Azure Traffic Manager can direct customers to their closest AKS cluster and application instance. For the best performance and redundancy, direct all application traffic through Traffic Manager before it goes to your AKS cluster.
items.description: Azure Traffic Manager can direct customers to their closest AKS cluster and application instance. For the best performance and redundancy, direct all application traffic through Traffic Manager before it goes to your AKS cluster.

items.text: With Azure Container Networking Interface (CNI), every pod gets an IP address from the subnet and can be accessed directly.
items.description: With Azure Container Networking Interface (CNI), every pod gets an IP address from the subnet and can be accessed directly.

items.text: To improve the security of your clusters and minimize the risk of attacks, we recommend limiting the IP address ranges that can access the API server.
items.description: To improve the security of your clusters and minimize the risk of attacks, we recommend limiting the IP address ranges that can access the API server.

items.text: Whilst AKS customers are able to route egress traffic through an Azure Load Balancer, there are limitations on the amount of outbound flows of traffic that is possible.
items.description: Whilst AKS customers are able to route egress traffic through an Azure Load Balancer, there are limitations on the amount of outbound flows of traffic that is possible.

items.text: A drawback with the traditional CNI is the exhaustion of pod IP addresses as the AKS cluster grows, resulting in the need to rebuild the entire cluster in a bigger subnet.
items.description: A drawback with the traditional CNI is the exhaustion of pod IP addresses as the AKS cluster grows, resulting in the need to rebuild the entire cluster in a bigger subnet.

items.text: The App Gateway Ingress Controller (AGIC) is a pod within your Kubernetes cluster. AGIC monitors the Kubernetes Ingress resources, and creates and applies App Gateway config based on these.
items.description: The App Gateway Ingress Controller (AGIC) is a pod within your Kubernetes cluster. AGIC monitors the Kubernetes Ingress resources, and creates and applies App Gateway config based on these.

items.text: This will help keep your cluster secure by limiting access from outside the virtual network to Azure PaaS services
items.description: This will help keep your cluster secure by limiting access from outside the virtual network to Azure PaaS services
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | where isnotnull(properties.apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster) | extend compliant = (properties.apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster==true) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: A service mesh provides capabilities like traffic management, resiliency, policy, security, strong identity, and observability to your workloads.
items.description: A service mesh provides capabilities like traffic management, resiliency, policy, security, strong identity, and observability to your workloads.

items.text: A service mesh provides capabilities like traffic management, resiliency, policy, security, strong identity, and observability to your workloads.
items.description: A service mesh provides capabilities like traffic management, resiliency, policy, security, strong identity, and observability to your workloads.

items.text: This can address requirements such as having non-contiguous virtual network address space to split across node pools and other security reasons.
items.description: This can address requirements such as having non-contiguous virtual network address space to split across node pools and other security reasons.

items.text: The Service CIDR should not overlap with Pod CIDR or Subnet CIDR or even peered VNET CIDR. If not configured correctly, the cluster might have strange behavior.
items.description: The Service CIDR should not overlap with Pod CIDR or Subnet CIDR or even peered VNET CIDR. If not configured correctly, the cluster might have strange behavior.

items.text: AKS supports upgrading the images on a node so you're up to date with the newest OS and runtime updates. AKS provides one new image per week with the latest updates, so it's beneficial to upgrade your node's images regularly for the latest features, including Linux or Windows patches
items.description: AKS supports upgrading the images on a node so you're up to date with the newest OS and runtime updates. AKS provides one new image per week with the latest updates, so it's beneficial to upgrade your node's images regularly for the latest features, including Linux or Windows patches

items.text: When deploying your application in Azure, spreading Virtual Machine (VM) instances across regions or availability zones creates network latency, which may impact the overall performance of your application. A proximity placement group is a logical grouping used to make sure Azure compute resources are physically located close to each other
items.description: When deploying your application in Azure, spreading Virtual Machine (VM) instances across regions or availability zones creates network latency, which may impact the overall performance of your application. A proximity placement group is a logical grouping used to make sure Azure compute resources are physically located close to each other

items.text: Cluster extensions provides an Azure Resource Manager driven experience for installation and lifecycle management of services like Azure Machine Learning (ML) on an AKS cluster.
items.description: Cluster extensions provides an Azure Resource Manager driven experience for installation and lifecycle management of services like Azure Machine Learning (ML) on an AKS cluster.

items.text: When creating a new AKS cluster, the MC_ resource group is created by default. You can customize the name of the MC_ resource group.
items.description: When creating a new AKS cluster, the MC_ resource group is created by default. You can customize the name of the MC_ resource group.

items.text: Don't expose remote connectivity to your AKS nodes. Create a bastion host, or jump box, in a management virtual network. Use the bastion host to securely route traffic into your AKS cluster to remote management tasks.
items.description: Don't expose remote connectivity to your AKS nodes. Create a bastion host, or jump box, in a management virtual network. Use the bastion host to securely route traffic into your AKS cluster to remote management tasks.

items.text: Regularly run the latest version of cluster scanning open source tool to detect issues in your cluster. For instance, if you apply resource quotas on an existing AKS cluster, run kubestriker first to find pods that don't have resource requests and limits defined.
items.description: Regularly run the latest version of cluster scanning open source tool to detect issues in your cluster. For instance, if you apply resource quotas on an existing AKS cluster, run kubestriker first to find pods that don't have resource requests and limits defined.

items.text: Ensure that you are always aware of what happens in your cluster. Monitor the health of the cluster (nodes, server) but also the pods
items.description: Ensure that you are always aware of what happens in your cluster. Monitor the health of the cluster (nodes, server) but also the pods

items.text: If default integration can collect telemetry data and basic metrics (CPU/Memory), they don't collect custom metrics and more detailed information. It's often necessary to install a 3rd party software (prometheus is recommend within Kubernetes) and they store these metrics to exploit them.
items.description: If default integration can collect telemetry data and basic metrics (CPU/Memory), they don't collect custom metrics and more detailed information. It's often necessary to install a 3rd party software (prometheus is recommend within Kubernetes) and they store these metrics to exploit them.

items.text: Distributed tracing, also called distributed request tracing, is a method used to profile and monitor applications, especially those built using a microservices architecture. Distributed tracing helps pinpoint where failures occur and what causes poor performance.
items.description: Distributed tracing, also called distributed request tracing, is a method used to profile and monitor applications, especially those built using a microservices architecture. Distributed tracing helps pinpoint where failures occur and what causes poor performance.

items.text: In addition to manually upgrading a cluster, you can set an auto-upgrade channel on your cluster
items.description: In addition to manually upgrading a cluster, you can set an auto-upgrade channel on your cluster

items.text: To keep up with application demands in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), you may need to adjust the number of nodes that run your workloads. The cluster autoscaler component can watch for pods in your cluster that can't be scheduled because of resource constraints.
items.description: To keep up with application demands in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), you may need to adjust the number of nodes that run your workloads. The cluster autoscaler component can watch for pods in your cluster that can't be scheduled because of resource constraints.

items.text: GitOps works by using Git as a single source of truth for declarative infrastructure and applications
items.description: GitOps works by using Git as a single source of truth for declarative infrastructure and applications

items.text: Instead of GitOps (or in addition) you can use CI/CD to deploy workloads/tools in your cluster
items.description: Instead of GitOps (or in addition) you can use CI/CD to deploy workloads/tools in your cluster

items.text: The Kubernetes ecosystem is strengthened by many tools that make operating it easier. Here's a few
items.description: The Kubernetes ecosystem is strengthened by many tools that make operating it easier. Here's a few

items.text: It's recommended to keep all applications in a namespace other than default
items.description: It's recommended to keep all applications in a namespace other than default

items.text: A common set of labels allows tools to work interoperably, describing objects in a common manner that all tools can understand. For instance, resources should have technical, business and security labels.
items.description: A common set of labels allows tools to work interoperably, describing objects in a common manner that all tools can understand. For instance, resources should have technical, business and security labels.

items.text: Customizing your node configuration allows you to configure or tune your operating system (OS) settings, node pools or the kubelet parameters to match the needs of the workloads.
items.description: Customizing your node configuration allows you to configure or tune your operating system (OS) settings, node pools or the kubelet parameters to match the needs of the workloads.

items.text: You can use command invoke to access private clusters without having to configure a VPN or Express Route
items.description: You can use command invoke to access private clusters without having to configure a VPN or Express Route

items.text: With Container insights, you can use the performance charts and health status to monitor the workload of Kubernetes clusters hosted on AKS
items.description: With Container insights, you can use the performance charts and health status to monitor the workload of Kubernetes clusters hosted on AKS

items.text: To help troubleshoot your application and services, you may need to view the logs generated by the master components. Be aware that if you don't enable these logs, there is no way for Microsoft to retrieve them for you
items.description: To help troubleshoot your application and services, you may need to view the logs generated by the master components. Be aware that if you don't enable these logs, there is no way for Microsoft to retrieve them for you

items.text: Optimizing node size can lead to lots of benefits including cost optimization, optimized performance and proper resource allocation
items.description: Optimizing node size can lead to lots of benefits including cost optimization, optimized performance and proper resource allocation

items.text: Azure advisor can enhance environment monitoring and can help improve the operations of your cluster by reducing costs, etc
items.description: Azure advisor can enhance environment monitoring and can help improve the operations of your cluster by reducing costs, etc

items.text: There are various tools that can make creating k8s manifest files much easier and can help with operational efficiency
items.description: There are various tools that can make creating k8s manifest files much easier and can help with operational efficiency

items.text: Periodically, you may need to rotate those certificates for security or policy reasons. For example, you may have a policy to rotate all your certificates every 90 days.
items.description: Periodically, you may need to rotate those certificates for security or policy reasons. For example, you may have a policy to rotate all your certificates every 90 days.

items.text: Monitoring the logs from your cluster can help prevent issues and identify areas of improvements
items.description: Monitoring the logs from your cluster can help prevent issues and identify areas of improvements
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(properties.addonProfiles.omsagent) and properties.addonProfiles.omsagent.enabled==true) | distinct id,compliant

items.text: ContainerLogV2 schema improves the data related to the container logs (such as namespace, pod name, container name, etc.) and allows you to query the logs using these fields
items.description: ContainerLogV2 schema improves the data related to the container logs (such as namespace, pod name, container name, etc.) and allows you to query the logs using these fields

items.text: Virtual nodes with ACI can help speed up scaling
items.description: Virtual nodes with ACI can help speed up scaling
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(properties.addonProfiles.aciConnectorLinux) and properties.addonProfiles.aciConnectorLinux.enabled==true) | distinct id,compliant

items.text: Spot nodepools can help with cost management
items.description: Spot nodepools can help with cost management

items.text: Alerts can provide early warnings of issues, etc in your cluster
items.description: Alerts can provide early warnings of issues, etc in your cluster

items.text: Monitoring these ports are important for security reasons
items.description: Monitoring these ports are important for security reasons

items.text: Learn how to troubleshoot resource health issues
items.description: Learn how to troubleshoot resource health issues

items.text: AKS emits certain events that enable it to subscribe to EventGrid which can be used for automation
items.description: AKS emits certain events that enable it to subscribe to EventGrid which can be used for automation

items.text: Develop own governance practices to make sure no changes are performed by operators in the node RG (aka 'infra RG')
items.description: Develop own governance practices to make sure no changes are performed by operators in the node RG (aka 'infra RG')

items.text: You are never ready when something bad happen and debugging a kubernetes cluster can be tricky. Some toolbox can be deployed to help you to find and fix the most common issues
items.description: You are never ready when something bad happen and debugging a kubernetes cluster can be tricky. Some toolbox can be deployed to help you to find and fix the most common issues

items.text: To rapidly scale your AKS cluster, you can integrate with Azure Container Instances (ACI)
items.description: To rapidly scale your AKS cluster, you can integrate with Azure Container Instances (ACI)

items.text: what type of worker nodes should I use, and how many of them is a critical question which requires the analysis of the workloads deployed on your cluster to get the best values of it
items.description: what type of worker nodes should I use, and how many of them is a critical question which requires the analysis of the workloads deployed on your cluster to get the best values of it

items.text: Benefit from ARM64's better price, compute performance, and lower power utilization in AKS node pools.
items.description: Benefit from ARM64's better price, compute performance, and lower power utilization in AKS node pools.

items.text: Plan and apply resource quotas at the namespace level. If pods don't define resource requests and limits, reject the deployment. Monitor resource usage and adjust quotas as needed.
items.description: Plan and apply resource quotas at the namespace level. If pods don't define resource requests and limits, reject the deployment. Monitor resource usage and adjust quotas as needed.

items.text: A LimitRange is a policy to constrain resource allocations (to Pods or Containers) in a namespace. It's useful to ensure that pods don't forget to declare request limits
items.description: A LimitRange is a policy to constrain resource allocations (to Pods or Containers) in a namespace. It's useful to ensure that pods don't forget to declare request limits

items.text: Set CPU and memory limits and requests to all the containers. It prevents memory leaks and CPU over-usage and protects the whole platform
items.description: Set CPU and memory limits and requests to all the containers. It prevents memory leaks and CPU over-usage and protects the whole platform

items.text: To maintain the availability of applications, define Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) to make sure that a minimum number of pods are available in the cluster.
items.description: To maintain the availability of applications, define Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) to make sure that a minimum number of pods are available in the cluster.

items.text: These tools can be very valuable for cost allocation especially in the case of multitenancy
items.description: These tools can be very valuable for cost allocation especially in the case of multitenancy

items.text: Nvidia's A100 GPU can be divided in up to seven independent instances. Each instance has their own memory and Stream Multiprocessor (SM).
items.description: Nvidia's A100 GPU can be divided in up to seven independent instances. Each instance has their own memory and Stream Multiprocessor (SM).

items.text: Your AKS workloads may not need to run continuously, for example a development cluster that has node pools running specific workloads.
items.description: Your AKS workloads may not need to run continuously, for example a development cluster that has node pools running specific workloads.

items.text: Node pool snapshots allow you to take a configuration snapshot of your node pool and then create new node pools or new clusters based of that snapshot for as long as that configuration and kubernetes version is supported.
items.description: Node pool snapshots allow you to take a configuration snapshot of your node pool and then create new node pools or new clusters based of that snapshot for as long as that configuration and kubernetes version is supported.

items.text: Azure VMs/nodes have quotas per subscription. Ensure that you have planned for these quota and proactively prepare to request for more if needed
items.description: Azure VMs/nodes have quotas per subscription. Ensure that you have planned for these quota and proactively prepare to request for more if needed

items.text: Scale-down Mode allows you to decide whether you would like to delete or deallocate the nodes in your Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster upon scaling down.
items.description: Scale-down Mode allows you to decide whether you would like to delete or deallocate the nodes in your Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster upon scaling down.

items.text: Understand the needs of your application to pick the right storage. Use high performance, SSD-backed storage for production workloads. Plan for network-based storage when there is a need for multiple concurrent connections.
items.description: Understand the needs of your application to pick the right storage. Use high performance, SSD-backed storage for production workloads. Plan for network-based storage when there is a need for multiple concurrent connections.

items.text: Each node size supports a maximum number of disks. Different node sizes also provide different amounts of local storage and network bandwidth. Plan for your application demands to deploy the appropriate size of nodes.
items.description: Each node size supports a maximum number of disks. Different node sizes also provide different amounts of local storage and network bandwidth. Plan for your application demands to deploy the appropriate size of nodes.

items.text: To reduce management overhead and let you scale, don't statically create and assign persistent volumes. Use dynamic provisioning. In your storage classes, define the appropriate reclaim policy to minimize unneeded storage costs once pods are deleted.
items.description: To reduce management overhead and let you scale, don't statically create and assign persistent volumes. Use dynamic provisioning. In your storage classes, define the appropriate reclaim policy to minimize unneeded storage costs once pods are deleted.

items.text: Back up your data using an appropriate tool for your storage type, such as Velero or Azure Site Recovery
items.description: Back up your data using an appropriate tool for your storage type, such as Velero or Azure Site Recovery

items.text: Where possible, don't store service state inside the container. Instead, use an Azure platform as a service (PaaS) that supports multiregion replication.
items.description: Where possible, don't store service state inside the container. Instead, use an Azure platform as a service (PaaS) that supports multiregion replication.

items.text: With ephemeral OS disks, you see lower read/write latency on the OS disk of AKS agent nodes since the disk is locally attached.
items.description: With ephemeral OS disks, you see lower read/write latency on the OS disk of AKS agent nodes since the disk is locally attached.
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | project id,resourceGroup,name,pools=properties.agentPoolProfiles | mvexpand pools | extend compliant = (pools.osDiskType=='Ephemeral') | project id,name=strcat(name,'-',pools.name), resourceGroup, compliant

items.text: Azure ultra disks offer high throughput, high IOPS, and consistent low latency disk storage for your stateful applications.
items.description: Azure ultra disks offer high throughput, high IOPS, and consistent low latency disk storage for your stateful applications.

items.text: Where possible, don't store service state inside the container. Instead, use an Azure platform as a service (PaaS) that supports multiregion replication.
items.description: Where possible, don't store service state inside the container. Instead, use an Azure platform as a service (PaaS) that supports multiregion replication.

items.text: If using Azure Disks and AZs, consider having nodepools within a zone for LRS disk with VolumeBindingMode:WaitForFirstConsumer for provisioning storage in right zone or use ZRS disk for nodepools spanning multiple zones
items.description: If using Azure Disks and AZs, consider having nodepools within a zone for LRS disk with VolumeBindingMode:WaitForFirstConsumer for provisioning storage in right zone or use ZRS disk for nodepools spanning multiple zones

items.text: For non-ephemeral disks, use high IOPS and larger OS disks for the nodes when running many pods/node since it requires high performance for running multiple pods and will generate huge logs with default AKS log rotation thresholds
items.description: For non-ephemeral disks, use high IOPS and larger OS disks for the nodes when running many pods/node since it requires high performance for running multiple pods and will generate huge logs with default AKS log rotation thresholds

items.text: Windows has strict compatibility rules, where the host OS version must match the container base image OS version. Only Windows containers with a container operating system of Windows Server 2019 are supported.
items.description: Windows has strict compatibility rules, where the host OS version must match the container base image OS version. Only Windows containers with a container operating system of Windows Server 2019 are supported.

items.text: TerminationGracePeriod is not implemented on Windows containers
items.description: TerminationGracePeriod is not implemented on Windows containers

items.text: Windows containers do not support elevation of privilege
items.description: Windows containers do not support elevation of privilege

items.text: There are no OOM eviction actions taken by the kubelet. Windows always treats all user-mode memory allocations as virtual, and pagefiles are mandatory. If memory is over-provisioned and all physical memory is exhausted, then paging can slow down performance.
items.description: There are no OOM eviction actions taken by the kubelet. Windows always treats all user-mode memory allocations as virtual, and pagefiles are mandatory. If memory is over-provisioned and all physical memory is exhausted, then paging can slow down performance.

items.text: AKS clusters with Windows node pools must use the Azure CNI (advanced) networking model. Kubenet (basic) networking is not supported.
items.description: AKS clusters with Windows node pools must use the Azure CNI (advanced) networking model. Kubenet (basic) networking is not supported.

items.text: Windows Server nodes in AKS must be upgraded to get the latest patch fixes and updates. Windows Updates are not enabled on nodes in AKS. AKS releases new node pool images as soon as patches are available, it is the customers responsibility to upgrade node pools to stay current on patches and hotfix.
items.description: Windows Server nodes in AKS must be upgraded to get the latest patch fixes and updates. Windows Updates are not enabled on nodes in AKS. AKS releases new node pool images as soon as patches are available, it is the customers responsibility to upgrade node pools to stay current on patches and hotfix.

items.text: Network policies are currently not supported, ensure that the containerized applications have a layer of protection like authentication
items.description: Network policies are currently not supported, ensure that the containerized applications have a layer of protection like authentication

items.text: Group Managed Service Accounts (GMSA) is a managed domain account for multiple servers that provides automatic password management, simplified service principal name (SPN) management and the ability to delegate the management to other administrators.
items.description: Group Managed Service Accounts (GMSA) is a managed domain account for multiple servers that provides automatic password management, simplified service principal name (SPN) management and the ability to delegate the management to other administrators.

items.text: HostProcess / Privileged containers extend the Windows container model to enable a wider range of Kubernetes cluster management scenarios.
items.description: HostProcess / Privileged containers extend the Windows container model to enable a wider range of Kubernetes cluster management scenarios.

items.text: Adding taints, tolerations and node selectors will help avoid having pods scheduled in the wrong OS node pool
items.description: Adding taints, tolerations and node selectors will help avoid having pods scheduled in the wrong OS node pool

items.text: For Windows 2019 and 2022 AKS nodes Calico Network Policies can be use
items.description: For Windows 2019 and 2022 AKS nodes Calico Network Policies can be use
items.graph: where type=='microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters' | where isnotnull(properties.apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster) | extend compliant = (properties.apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster==true) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: For Windows workloads use Accelerated Networking
items.description: For Windows workloads use Accelerated Networking
